description: Tracks which bubble categories have been audited/expanded by agents, so future sessions know what's been reviewed and what still needs attention.

translation.notes: 5 language pairs x 5 tones = 25 leaves. Deep 3-level nesting. No changes needed now.

read.notes: Restructured: removed analysis modes (summary, keypoints, fullAnalysis) that overlapped with summarize. Now 9 children: 5 primary source types + 4 secondary (contract, spreadsheet, compare, factcheck).

organize.notes: 10 children, well-balanced. No changes needed now.

summarize.notes: Expanded from 5 to 8 children. Added podcast, legal, conversation.

debug.notes: Expanded from 5 to 8 children. Added performance, security, mobile as secondary.

brainstorm.notes: Expanded from 5 to 10 children. Added naming, event, problemSolving, startup, sideProject as secondary.

learn.notes: Expanded from 5 to 10 children. Added history, design, music, cooking, finance as secondary.

write.notes: Expanded from 5 to 10 children. Added socialMedia, resume, report, copy, speech as secondary.

explain.notes: Expanded from 5 to 8 children. Added data, math, legal as secondary.

structural_issues.issue: read category mixes source types and analysis modes
structural_issues.recommendation: Remove read-summary, read-keypoints, read-full; add read-contract, read-spreadsheet

structural_issues.issue: write-email is too broad for one of the most common AI use cases
build-website and build-app could benefit from sub-branches
structural_issues.recommendation: Split website into landing/portfolio/ecommerce; app into mobile/desktop/web

changes: write: +5 leaves (socialMedia, resume, report, copy, speech)
brainstorm: +5 leaves (naming, event, problemSolving, startup, sideProject)
learn: +5 leaves (history, design, music, cooking, finance)
read: restructured — removed 3 analysis-mode leaves (summary, keypoints, fullAnalysis), added 2 source-type leaves (contract, spreadsheet)
